A Few Tricks for Wrapping localStorage So It Stops Annoying You

When you're building web apps, you reach for localStorage to store data all the time — but the localStorage API itself is honestly kind of clunky. In this post I'll show a few tricks for wrapping it so it's nicer to use, covering both the Function and Class approaches, plus how to dodge the usual JSON.parse errors.
Token in localStorage? sessionStorage? Or Cookie?
This post is about some things I've picked up about JWT Tokens — and why you shouldn't store JWT Tokens in localStorage.
Local Dev Hits "Failed to read localStorage: Access is denied" — Here' s the Fix

Hit a weird Chrome console error during local dev — "Uncaught DOMException: Failed to read the 'localStorage' property from 'Window': Access is denied for this document." Writing it down. Turns out Chrome's third-party cookie blocking is treating http://localhost:5500 as blocked. The fix is allowing the domain in Chrome's site settings — and as a bonus, the same setup works fine via 127.0.0.1:5500.
